lede: A look at August 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Bertalan Farkas and Stefan Effenberg, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Pakistan
events.desc: Pakistan is re-admitted to the Commonwealth of Nations after having restored democracy for the first time since 1972.

events.title: Summerland disaster
events.desc: A flash fire kills 50 people at the Summerland amusement centre at Douglas, Isle of Man.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: After the Motor Torpedo Boat PT-109 is rammed and sunk by the Japanese destroyer Amagiri, Lt. John F. Kennedy, future U.S. president, saves all but two of his crew.

events.title: Albert Einstein
events.desc: Albert Einstein and Leo Szilard write a letter to Franklin D. Roosevelt, urging him to begin the Manhattan Project to develop a nuclear weapon.

events.title: Calvin Coolidge
events.desc: U.S. Vice President Calvin Coolidge becomes president upon the death of President Warren G. Harding.

events.title: Austria-Hungary
events.desc: World War I: Austrian sabotage causes the sinking of the Italian battleship Leonardo da Vinci in Taranto.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The German occupation of Luxembourg begins.

events.title: Tower Subway
events.desc: Tower Subway, the world's first underground tube railway, opens in London, England, United Kingdom.

events.title: 1790 United States census
events.desc: The first United States Census is conducted.

events.title: Julius Caesar
events.desc: Caesar, who marched to Spain earlier in the year, leaving Marcus Antonius in charge of Italy, defeats Pompey's general Afranius and Petreius in Ilerda (Lerida) north of the Ebro river.

births.name: Shimon Peres
births.line: Polish-Israeli lawyer and politician, 9th President of Israel

deaths.text: Fritz Lang, Austrian-American director, producer, and screenwriter
Paul von Hindenburg, German field marshal and politician, 2nd president of Germany
Warren G. Harding, American journalist and politician, 29th president of the United States
Alexander Graham Bell, Scottish-Canadian engineer, invented the telephone
Oswald von Wolkenstein, Austrian poet and composer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Basil Fool for Christ (Russian Orthodox Church)
